associatedMedia.headline: Saint Conan's Kirk, Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland

Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alyse Margaret Crawford於1260年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特時，她的父親John Crawford15歲，母親Helen Hadden20歲。她於1279年與Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1310年在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍享年50歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾可雷南。

nameMeanings.name: Crawford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various places called Crawford, primarily the one in Lanarkshire (Scotland), and possibly also from the one in Lancashire. Both are named in Old English with crāwe ‘crow’ + ford ‘ford’.

variants.name: Crafford
meanings: English (Cumberland) and Scottish: variant of   Crawford  .

variants.name: Crofoot
meanings: English: variant ofCrowfoot, a nickname for someone with splayed feet or someother deformity of the foot, from Old English crāwe ‘crow’ +fōt ‘foot’. In Middle English crou-fot also denotedthe buttercup, and it may be from this sense that the name arose,although the reason for its adoption is unclear.

variants.name: Crofut
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of Crawford or Crowfoot (see Crofoot ).

nameMeanings.name: Alyse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Alyse Margaret Crawford was born in 1260, in Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, John Crawford, was 15 and her mother, Helen Hadden, was 20. She married Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow in 1279, in Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1310, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 50, and was buried in Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alyse Margaret Crawford est née en 1260, à Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Crawford, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Helen Hadden, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow en 1279, à Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1310, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alyse Margaret Crawfordが1260年にイギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトで生まれた時，父John Crawfordは15歳，母Helen Haddenは20歳であった。1279年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルでSir Neil Campbell of Lochowと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1310年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャウで50歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトキルチレナンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alyse Margaret Crawford(이)가 1260년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Crawford(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helen Hadden(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1279년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이에서 Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1310년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭아w에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬크리난에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow em 1279, em Argyll, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.Church of St. Peter the Deacon Kilchrena: килчренан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alyse Margaret Crawford родилась в 1260 г. в местности аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Crawford было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen Hadden, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow в 1279 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1310 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности килчренан, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alyse Margaret Crawford nació en 1260, en Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, John Crawford, tenía 15 años y su mamá, Helen Hadden, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow en 1279, en Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1310, en Lochawe, Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Kilchrenan, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido.
